title: Spicy Gooey Chocolate Stir-fry
description: A lazy-sunday mexican-inspired dish featuring a spicy chocolate sauce made with ras el hanout and bhuna spices for an added kick, perfect for vegans or pescatarians.

ingredients.name: Ras el Hanout Spice Mix
ingredients.notes: Can be found in Middle Eastern grocery stores or online

ingredients.name: Bhuna Spice Paste
ingredients.notes: A blend of curry powder, cumin, coriander seeds, and chili paste

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced finely for a milder flavor

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral oil like avocado or grapeseed for a healthier option

instructions: In a small saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add ras el hanout and bhuna spice paste. Stir until fragrant and spices are coated.
Add garlic and chili flakes to the pan, stirring for about 30 seconds or until they start to soften and release their flavors.
Gradually add water while continuously stirring with a wooden spoon to prevent burning.
Once it starts boiling, reduce heat to low and stir in dark chocolate. Cook, stirring frequently, until mixture is smooth and thickened (about 5 minutes).
Sprinkle nutritional yeast on top of the sauce for extra texture and flavor. Let simmer for another minute or two until everything is well combined.
Pour the mixture into a shallow baking dish lined with parchment paper to prevent sticking. Bake in preheated oven for about 10-15 minutes or until golden brown.
Remove from heat and let it cool slightly before serving.

axes.descriptor: Spicy Gooey Lazy Sunday Pescatarian Friendly
